data.title: Controlled Scheduling Without Platform Lock-In
data.summary: Booking systems directly influence conversion, capacity planning, and operational flow. Yet in many platforms, scheduling logic is embedded in backend templates, tightly coupled to session state, and difficult to evolve safely. What drives revenue often becomes a source of deployment anxiety.

sections.title: Where friction appears
items: Booking changes risk breaking unrelated pages
Capacity adjustments are slow to deploy
Experimenting with booking flows feels unsafe
Operational teams depend on engineering cycles

sections.title: What isolation changes
items: Booking rules evolve independently from theme code
Availability logic updated without platform risk
State contained within a predictable boundary
Conversion flow isolated from core templates

data.closing: Revenue-critical scheduling becomes controlled — not fragile.
